summary: Iteration 7 — Security hardening + CMS font/aspect controls. Backend: authored /app/backend/tests/test_security_hardening.py with 12 tests, ALL PASS. Covers (a) rate limiting on 4 endpoints — /api/newsletter (10/min), /api/contact (5/min), /api/auth/session (15/min), /api/reservations (20/min) — verifying correct 429 threshold + Retry-After header + row cleanup; (b) admin gating on /api/seed and /api/cms/seed (401 anon / 403 user / 200 admin returning already-seeded response); (c) INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL bootstrap — daniel.ovidiu@gmail.com has role='admin' in DB and 'Bootstrapped daniel.ovidiu@gmail.com to admin' appears in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. Regression suite (backend_test.py + test_refactor_regression.py + test_daniel_admin_rbac.py + test_cms.py): 52/53 pass on first run — the 1 failure was a stale test in test_cms.py::test_seed_idempotent that expected /api/cms/seed to be public (it is now admin-gated by design); updated the test to pass admin auth → now 53/53. Frontend: minted admin cookie, created a hidden /p/security-test page via API with a custom_html block containing `Safe` and loaded it in the browser — DOMPurify strips the script (window.evilRan===undefined, no
critical_code_review_comments: rate_limit() in server.py is in-memory + per-worker. Fine for the current single-container MVP, but if this ever runs behind >1 uvicorn worker or >1 pod, each process will keep its own bucket and the effective limit becomes N × configured limit. Note in code comment already flags this — recommend swapping for Redis-backed limiter before horizontal scaling.

rate_limit() uses the FIRST value in X-Forwarded-For. Behind ingress this is the real client IP but an attacker who controls the X-Forwarded-For header directly can trivially bypass by rotating the first entry. Consider trusting only the LAST hop the ingress adds (or a fixed offset from the right).

cms_routes.py line 194 admin_patch_theme: body.draft is stored raw with no schema validation — an editor could set draft={colors:{bg:'javascript:alert(1)'}} which then goes through applyTheme() and gets written as a CSS var. Not directly exploitable (CSS var value isn't executed) but tightening the ThemePatch model to a nested Pydantic schema would be safer.

CustomHTML block DOMPurify config in components/blocks/index.jsx line 286 uses default sanitization. Verify it also strips `on*` inline event handlers (default DOMPurify config does). Test attempted covered
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_cms.py (test_seed_idempotent updated to send admin Bearer token — /api/cms/seed is now admin-gated by design)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 18/18 assertions (DOMPurify, font inputs, font loading, 4 aspect selects, 3 public routes)

test_credentials: Test tokens minted via mongosh were used and then cleaned up in teardown (db.users.deleteMany({email:/@umbra.test$/}); db.user_sessions.deleteMany({session_token:/^test_/})). daniel.ovidiu@gmail.com was NOT touched — role verified as 'admin' via read-only findOne. If the next iteration needs admin/editor/user tokens, mint them fresh via the mongosh snippet in /app/auth_testing.md.
seed_data_creation: Temporary /p/security-test CMS page was created for DOMPurify test then deleted. Newsletter TEST_rl_* + contact TEST_rl_* rows created by rate-limit tests were cleaned up inline. No seeded production data was modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 rate limits verified with the actual thresholds (10 newsletter, 5 contact, 15 auth/session, 20 reservations). Buckets are in-memory sliding window keyed by IP — if you rerun the same rate-limit test within 60s without restarting the backend, you WILL start at the already-elevated count and see the 11th/6th/etc bucket earlier than expected. Solution: sudo supervisorctl restart backend between reruns, OR (better) rerun individual endpoint tests since each endpoint has its own bucket key. The test_security_hardening.py suite was designed to run in a single sequential pass without needing between-test sleeps because each class targets a different endpoint bucket. The regression suite test_cms.py::test_seed_idempotent was updated (previously assumed public /api/cms/seed). Iteration 7 ships clean — no bugs found, no code changes to production files were needed.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Only stale-test cleanup: test_cms.py::test_seed_idempotent was pre-security-hardening and hit the now-401 endpoint without auth. Fixed by adding admin Bearer header.
